description: Route handler now calls the database directly, violating documented thin-handler policy. Should BLOCK or WARN on architecture.

input.staged_diff:
diff --git a/routes/checkout.py b/routes/checkout.py
index 1a2b3c4..5d6e7f8 100644
--- a/routes/checkout.py
+++ b/routes/checkout.py
@@ -1,7 +1,11 @@
 from flask import request, jsonify
-from services.order_service import process_order
+import psycopg2
 
 def checkout():
     data = request.json
-    result = process_order(data)
+    conn = psycopg2.connect(dsn='postgresql://db:5432/orders')
+    cur = conn.cursor()
+    cur.execute('INSERT INTO orders VALUES (%s, %s)', (data['user_id'], data['total']))
+    conn.commit()
+    result = {'order_id': cur.lastrowid}
     return jsonify(result)
input.policy_file: ARCH.md contains: 'Route handlers must remain thin. All database access must go through service classes in services/.'
